decision.baseline: packed_load remains the best kernel and the adopted device baseline (+6%/+2% over v1_partial)
decision.other_variants: bfe_nibble and lut_dequant are also ALU-level changes; given iteration 1's lesson they are low-probability and were not pursued without a new hypothesis
decision.residual_gap: the remaining ~2-5x is not reachable by dequant-ALU restructuring; closing it needs a different attack -- a reduction/occupancy structure that adds parallelism without serializing, a different storage layout, or a fused decode+matmul -- or is substantially irreducible for this kernel approach on this latency-bound GEMV

iteration_1.correctness: PASS (exact numerics) on attn_q and ffn_gate
iteration_1.idea: decode _q4k_group_params once per group; factor d*sc and dmin*mn out of the 32-element sum (contrib = d*sc*sum(q*x) - dmin*mn*sum(x))
iteration_1.why: collapsing pos/lane4 into a full unroll bloats the body (MORE ALU, not less) and serializes the reduce; the saved decode is dwarfed

lesson: the bottleneck (M0b) is occupancy/latency, NOT redundant decode op-count; restructuring the reduce to hoist the decode backfires because reduce parallelism dominates. ALU-op reduction is the wrong lever.
note: G0'' iteration 1 recorded as an honest negative. The hoist_scale_min mode is kept in extra/q4_k_gemv_primitive.py as a documented, correct-but-slow result.
